title: I Gaed A Waefu' Gate Yestreen.
content:
Air - "The blue-eyed lass."


I.

        I gaed a waefu' gate yestreen,
            A gate, I fear, I'll dearlie rue;
        I gat my death frae twa sweet een,
            Twa lovely een o' bonnie blue.
        'Twas not her golden ringlets bright;
            Her lips, like roses, wat wi' dew,
        Her heaving bosom, lily-white,
            It was her een sae bonnie blue.

II.

        She talk'd, she smil'd, my heart she wyl'd;
            She charm'd my soul, I wist na how:
        And ay the stound, the deadly wound,
            Cam frae her een sae bonnie blue.
        But spare to speak, and spare to speed;
            She'll aiblins listen to my vow:
        Should she refuse, I'll lay my dead
            To her twa een sae bonnie blue.
meaning:
Robert Burns's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "I Gaed A Waefu' Gate Yestreen."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
